title: Path Traversal
description: An endpoint that serves files from a directory based on a filename parameter.

stages.name: Find the vulnerability
stages.instruction: Identify the vulnerability and what sensitive files could be accessed on a Linux system.
stages.target: The application doesn't sanitize the 'name' parameter, allowing an attacker to use '../' to traverse out of the 'uploads' directory. Sensitive files include /etc/passwd.

stages.name: Exploit it
stages.instruction: Provide a payload to read the /etc/passwd file.

stages.name: Patch it
stages.instruction: How would you fix this using 'os.path.basename'?

stages.name: Explain it
stages.instruction: Explain how 'os.path.basename' helps prevent path traversal.
stages.target: It extracts only the filename part of a path, stripping away any directory components like '../'.

hints: Think about how you navigate directories in a terminal.
